name: Persuasion Engine
description: Can an AI choose the right persuasion strategy for a given person? Persuasion Engine benchmarks this directly. A model must convince a scripted NPC across 25 turns, but the NPC has a hidden resistance profile — only one of six argument types moves them effectively. The model must infer the right strategy from NPC response language and commit to it. 23 scenarios isolate distinct capabilities: logical reasoning, emotional intelligence, authority recognition, social consensus, narrative storytelling, and concession-first trust-building. Metrics: win_rate (did it convince?), strategy_optimality (did it choose well?), avg_turns_to_win (how fast?). Showcase: https://evanly-gh.github.io/Sweccathon-2026/

binding_vow.description: The agent plays a persuader trying to shift an NPC's opinion on a real-world topic. Each episode features a different NPC archetype with a hidden resistance profile — some respond to logic, others to emotion, social proof, authority, personal anecdotes, or concessions. The agent sees only the NPC's reply text and must infer the hidden type. Repeating the same argument type each turn incurs a growing penalty. The episode ends when the NPC's internal agreement crosses a hidden threshold (win), when turns run out (loss), or if the NPC closes the conversation. 15 seeded scenarios span easy to very hard.

observation_space.description: JSON object with: turn (int), max_turns (int), topic (str), your_position (str), npc_response (str — the NPC's latest reply), turns_remaining (str). First turn also contains scenario_context and npc_opening_statement and instruction.

action_space.description: JSON object with a single key 'message': your persuasive argument to the NPC (string, 10–300 chars). Be concise — one focused argument per turn. Strategy matters: the NPC responds differently to emotional appeals (EMOTIONAL), logical evidence (LOGICAL), peer behavior (SOCIAL_PROOF), expert opinion (AUTHORITY), personal stories (ANECDOTE), or genuine acknowledgment of their position (CONCESSION). Repeating the same approach each turn lowers its effectiveness.

reward.description: Per-turn agreement delta from NPC update, plus terminal bonus (+0.5 base + 0.05 per turn remaining) for winning. Negative when the NPC becomes more resistant. Normalised at episode end against do-nothing and greedy baselines baked into each scenario.
